hero.title: 5 Days, Kerala & the Hills.
hero.titleAccent: Backwaters, Spice Country and Cool Coimbatore.
hero.intro: From the harbour town of Cochin to a houseboat night in Alleppey and on to the gateway hills around Coimbatore. Five days that blend Kerala's backwaters with the cool, green edge of the Western Ghats.

overview.heading: 5 Days Cochin, Alleppey & Coimbatore: The Full Picture
body: This 5 day journey strings together three distinct sides of the region: the colonial harbour of Cochin, the serene backwaters of Alleppey, and Coimbatore, the cotton city at the foot of the Western Ghats that serves as a gateway to hill country.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip pairs an overnight Alleppey houseboat with the heritage of Fort Kochi and the cooler, greener landscapes near Coimbatore. Your own vehicle and driver guide make the transitions smooth and the pace relaxed.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Alleppey Backwater Houseboat
items.body: Spend a night on a private houseboat drifting through the Alleppey backwaters, with fresh Kerala meals cooked onboard and village life floating past.

items.title: Fort Kochi Heritage
items.body: The old harbour quarter's Chinese fishing nets, colonial churches and spice markets make a characterful start to the trip.

items.title: Coimbatore & the Western Ghats
items.body: Coimbatore, the gateway to the Nilgiri hills, offers temples, textile heritage and the cool green edge of the Western Ghats.

days.title: Arrive Cochin
days.description: Met at Cochin airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the fishing nets.

days.title: Cochin to Alleppey Houseboat
days.description: Morning Kochi sights, then board your Alleppey houseboat.

days.title: Alleppey to Coimbatore
days.description: Morning cruise, then a scenic drive to Coimbatore.

days.title: Coimbatore Sightseeing
days.description: Explore Coimbatore's temples and surroundings at the foot of the Ghats.

days.title: Depart Coimbatore
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Coimbatore airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Kerala Plus the Ghats, Linked Seamlessly
whyBook.body: Combining the backwaters with Coimbatore takes good routing and timing. We link Cochin, Alleppey and Coimbatore with a private vehicle so the journey feels effortless. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Cochin, Alleppey & Coimbatore Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day trip is ideal for travellers who want Kerala's famous backwaters plus a taste of the cooler hill country gateway at Coimbatore. The route blends heritage, a houseboat night and green Western Ghats scenery.

A private vehicle and driver guide make the multi-stop journey smooth and relaxed, with the Alleppey houseboat as the clear highlight.

For more hill country and tea estates, see our Munnar and Thekkady Kerala packages.

sections.heading: Why Add Coimbatore to a Kerala Trip
sections.body:
Coimbatore sits at the foot of the Western Ghats and acts as a gateway to the Nilgiri hills. It is known for its textile industry, temples like Marudhamalai, and the towering Adiyogi Shiva statue at the Isha Yoga Center.

It also offers a convenient airport, making it a practical end point for a Kerala loop that includes the backwaters.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Cochin or Coimbatore Airport (COK / CJB). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: September to March offers the most pleasant weather across both Kerala and the Coimbatore hills. The monsoon brings lush greenery but heavy rain. Coimbatore is generally cooler than coastal Kerala thanks to its position near the Ghats.

items.question: Does the trip start and end in different cities?
items.answer: It typically begins in Cochin and ends in Coimbatore, both of which have airports. We arrange flights and transfers to suit. Let us know your preferred routing when booking.

items.question: Is the houseboat included?
items.answer: Yes. The package includes a private overnight houseboat on the Alleppey backwaters, with a crew cooking fresh Kerala meals onboard, which is the highlight of the trip for most travellers.
